clinical_history: What two arrhythmias are present in this ECG from a middle-aged male?
diagnosis: Atrial fibrillation (with a moderate ventricular response) and frequent premature ventricular complexes (PVCs).  The PVCs are multifocal, with two distinct morphologies.  The ECG also shows borderline low limb lead voltage and a Q-wave in lead III, both non-diagnostic findings. (Prior inferior MI cannot be ruled out.)
